Class PasswordHashRecords

java.lang.Object
com.jamii.jUser.controller.PasswordHashRecords
@Component public class PasswordHashRecords extends Object
Service component for managing password hash records in the database.

This class tracks password history for users, enforcing password reuse policies by checking against the last 10 password hashes.

Key functionality:

  • Add new password hash records for users
  • Check if a password has been used in the last 10 records
  • Save password hash records individually or in batch

    ArrayList<PasswordHashRecordsTBL>
    dataList
    List of password hash records for batch operations.

  • Field Details

    • data

  • Method Details

    • addUserNewPasswordRecord

      public PasswordHashRecordsTBL addUserNewPasswordRecord(UserLoginTBL userLoginTBL)
      Adds a new password hash record for a user.

      This method captures the current password salt from the user's login record and stores it with a timestamp for password history tracking.

      Parameters:
      userLoginTBL - the user to add the password record for
      Returns:
      the saved password hash record with generated ID

    • isPasswordInLastTenRecords

      public Boolean isPasswordInLastTenRecords(UserLoginTBL userLoginTBL)
      Checks if the user's current password has been used in their last 10 password records.

      This method enforces password reuse policies by comparing the current password salt against the last 10 password hashes stored for the user.

      Parameters:
      userLoginTBL - the user to check password history for
      Returns:
      true if the password was used in the last 10 records, false otherwise

    • save

      public void save()
      Saves the current password hash record from the data field to the database.

      The saved entity (with generated ID) is stored back in the data field.

    • saveAll

      public void saveAll()
      Saves all password hash records in the dataList to the database in a batch operation.

      After saving, the list is cleared and repopulated with the saved entities (including any generated IDs).
